description: RT appears in the equation for the collision rate related to pA.
zA is derived based on the ratio of v^2 and A.
The desorption rate f_des depends on q, h, and Pd.
The rate of change of h (dh/dt) is governed by g.
g is defined as the difference between f_ads and f_des.
K is defined in terms of h_eq, q, and pA.
s is derived based on the relationship involving g and K across cycles.
The rate of change of pA involves both spatial and temporal derivatives.
xp is defined based on the overall diffusion process in the channel.
D is given as a function of the equilibrium constant K and A.
M links the quantities involved in the calculation of q.
h is dependent on time through an integral expression involving q, pA, and Pd.
The relationship defines variables as part of the force calculation.
D results from the real-time distance description in the channel.
